channel.title: Home on ijadux2's website
channel.description: Recent content in Home on ijadux2's website

item.title: Nixos
item.description:
Nxios

I use Nixos as my daily driver Operating System and i Love it.

Benefits of Nix-configured System

  • Whole OS in some declerative files .nix
  • esay to configure if you know
  • freedom to choose and config
  • make custome modules and pkgs for your setup

Disadvantages

  • Leraning curve extreme
  • unique errors and confusing
  • time to setup
  • learn the basics of linux, nix-lang, nix-pkgs etc

conclution

after all Nixos wins from Archlinux not just in terms of customizations, built for stability with Looks, manageable.

item.title: Indian_culture
item.description:
Indian_culture

Today 2026-03-06 16:45 i was watching this movie which was released in 2017 in India. called Kung fu yoga the director of this movie is Chinese respected Stanley Tong and famous Jackie Chan. Jackie Chan was the main leading character of the story, and there were many Indian actors and actoress as well,

main story

the story focus on the largest and the oldest Empire Magadh presently India, “Bihar”, and our Indian culture amoug with Chinese technology.

item.title: Neovim
item.description:
Neovim

I use Neovim mostly for everything.

  • applauncher: i have create a plugin for my neovim to launch apps , included flatpaksetc

  • git management:

i use git for my all project i has a clear pickup ui using snack.nvim picker
to chage branches , commit and push , push to remote etc

  • browseing

i make a custome plugin for browsing in neovim and out of neovim using w3m and external links for duckduckgo and youtube for external browser

item.title: craze
item.description:
craze

At this point in my life, in India today 2026-03-04 we celebrate our festival Holi festival of love, colours.

But since in this modern world no one cares about other as personal, today!! no colour, no meets-ups, no nothing the kid which used to spread and apply colour to his friends and family members is sitting in the room, waiting for his life to rest kind of other kids..

item.title: Opensource
item.description:
The Open Source Philosophy: Freedom, AI, and Community

To me, open source is more than a development model; it is a global social network of intellect. It connects minds across borders, proving that when we share our “bricks,” we can build cathedrals that no single person could create alone.

Beyond Freedom: The Power of “Wings”

Many talk about the “freedom” of open source, but for me, it is about empowerment. It gives me the wings to experiment, to fail, and to eventually soar.

item.title: Future
item.description:
Beyond the Stethoscope: Why I’m Choosing Code Over Clinicals

In the landscape of a traditional Indian household, career paths often feel like a binary choice: 0 (Doctor) or 1 (Engineer). For as long as I can remember, the “expected” path for me was the one leading to a medical degree. But while the world saw me in a white coat, I was busy staring at a terminal screen, falling in love with the logic of syntax and the thrill of a successful build.

item.title: Window Manager
item.description:
Hyprland and Sway

i like both of them and personaly i like hyprland not because of large community but for a good wayland compositor with in built blur and decoration.

and for sway i like it minimalism no heary dependencies ready in 20 to 30 mins

Hyprland vs. Sway: The Wayland Showdown

If you are moving away from X11, you are likely looking at these two powerhouses. Both are Tiling Window Managers (TWMs) built for Wayland, but they offer very different philosophies for your desktop experience.

item.title: Neovim and journey of coding
item.description:
Neovim

i use neovim for most for the time in my laptop. using archlinux with Noctalia shell, custome plugins and config

  • Sharingan.nvim

  • my neovim consist of

    • plugin managaer
    • theme {catppuccin-mocha}
    • my custome plugins {git,app-launcher,tangle,etc}

  • github plugins

    • my fav. snacks.nvim
    • and many more

just not becasue of plugins i like neovim i like becasue of speed and terminal based editor

my coding journey

when i was introduced by linux in 2020 it was hard for me to use

item.title: 🛠️ Developer Resource Hub
item.description:
🚀 Programming & Documentation

Resource Category Description/Link
DevDocs Tool devdocs.io
Zig Language ziglang.org
Ziggy Practice ijadux2/ziggy (Zig practice)
Nixi Language nixi-phi.vercel.app (Documentation)
Nixi Repo GitHub ijadux2/nixi
ADAN-lang Collab Cappucina/ADAN (Contribution needed)
linux kernel linux-kernel (github)
Arch linux Arch-wiki

Automation and Devops

python go javascript typescript lua bash zig c c++ c3 rust

Renne-script and Renne-editor

  • editor based on neovim and vim
  • configuration lang Renne-script

Messsaging app

  • built in zig
  • encrypted,foucs like telegram

Gitstack and Gitstack-app

  • https://github.com/Gitstack
  • https://github.com/Gitstack-app gui => gtk, cli, github-auth
  • http://localhost:2020/

👤 Personal Projects (@ijadux2) {Kumar Vaibhav}

Project Stack Link
Candy Crush Lua Candy Crush Repo (Game in Lua)
Libs-for-bash Bash Bash Libs Repo (Extra libs)
Nix Config NixOS Nix Dotfiles (Dotfiles)
Python Browser Python Python Browser Repo
Rin Nix nix-experiment (Web Framework - Private)
My Fedora System fedora Dotfiles (Fedora makeover)
Portfolio Web Portfolio Site (Personal Site)
nixi nixi nixi-lang (nixi language)

❄️ Nix Ecosystem

Core Repositories

  • NixOS Organization
  • Nix Package Manager
  • Nixpkgs
  • Nix.dev Documentation

Community Tools

Tool Description
Home-Manager nix-community/home-manager
Awesome-Nix nix-community/awesome-nix
NixVim nix-community/nixvim (Neovim + Nix)
Stylix nix-community/stylix
NixD nix-community/nixd

⌨️ Workflow & Editors

Neovim

  • Dotfyle
  • Neovim GitHub
  • My Config

Emacs user {tsoding}

  • Tsoding: GitHub
  • sowon Sowon
  • Rexim: GitHub
  • lust2d github replacemnt for love2d in js and raylib
  • game github nob.h

🎨 Aesthetic & Systems

  • Theme: Catppuccin Mocha
  • Fonts: Monaspace and Nerd Fonts

Distros:

  • NixOS
  • Garuda
  • Fedora

Inspiration: Tony-btw

item.title: About Me
item.description:
Hello, I’m Kumar Vaibhav

  • love to build
  • hands in config management
  • shell scripting
  • window managers
  • leraning Linux architecture
  • student

About Me

  • 💻 Using Neovim as my main editor
  • Linux user since - 🐧2020
  • 🔧 Building custom configurations and sharing them

Skills

  • Bash - Shell scripting for automation
  • Lua - Neovim plugin development
  • Python - Versatile programming
  • Linux - System administration
  • Nix - Declarative package management
  • Window Managers - Hyprland, Sway

Projects

Linux Development Environment

⚡ Neovim Configuration

Architecting a modular IDE experience. Built with Sharingan.nvim for maximum performance, this configuration transforms Neovim into a heavyweight development environment. It focuses on lightning-fast startup times, intuitive keybindings, and a decoupled plugin architecture.

item.title: Mental health
item.description:
Mental health

Expressing the reality of mental health as a teenager is incredibly difficult. You’re expected to have it all figured out while your brain is still under construction. Throwing a relationship into that mix? That’s a whole different level of complexity.

Honestly, trying to manage family expectations, a girlfriend’s needs, and a social life all at once is a genuine art form. Add a full day of staring at a screen, losing yourself in lines of code, and you’re running on empty before the day is even half over.
